data.title: Atlassian to acquire The Browser Company
data.description:
• Atlassian (NASDAQ:TEAM) said on Thursday that it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire The Browser Company, the company behind the Dia and Arc browsers. 
  • Under the terms of the definitive agreement, Atlassian will acquire The Browser Company for approximately $610M in cash, inclusive of The Browser Company’s cash balance. 
  • The transaction will be funded through cash from Atlassian’s balance sheet and is expected to close in the second quarter of its fiscal year 2026. 
  • The acquisition is not expected to have a material impact on the company’s financials in fiscal years 2026 and 2027.

data.title: Williams-Sonoma may have an edge on peers in implementing AI across its business
data.description:
Williams-Sonoma (NYSE:WSM) is emerging as one of the AI application leaders in the retail industry.

Notably, Williams-Sonoma (NYSE:WSM) has expanded its AI-powered customer service assistant across all of its brands, updated Jefferies analyst Corey Tarlowe. He said the retailer is aiming to improve resolution speed and reduce service costs. In addition, the company also previewed an upcoming AI culinary companion tool designed to improve product discovery and offer entertaining tips. Management discussed using AI to deepen personalization across its multichannel customer experience while leveraging data to improve issue resolution and reduce operating costs.

During Williams-Sonoma's (WSM) second-quarter earnings call, the topic of AI was front and center. The expectation is that there will be further opportunity for supply chain efficiencies in the long term as AI capabilities are implemented.

"We don't view AI as a standalone function," highlighted CEO Laura Alber. "It is embedded across our business," she added. The company has AI organized to enhance the customer experience, optimize the supply chain, and automate internal operations. 

"On the customer experience, we are already seeing strong results from our new AI-powered customer service assistant, which we launched with Pottery Barn Kids earlier this summer and are scaling across all of our brands this week. It is improving issue resolution rates and speed while reducing cost, creating a better experience for both customers and associates."

Looking ahead, Williams-Sonoma (WSM) plans to use a proprietary AI platform to rapidly create secure business-specific applications and automate more workflows across functions such as finance, HR, and technology.

Shares of Williams-Sonoma (WSM) are up 38% since they bottomed out in April amid broad tariff concerns.

data.title: DLocal prices 15M share secondary offering by General Atlantic Affiliate
data.description:
• DLocal (NASDAQ:DLO) priced a 15M Class A share secondary offering at $12.75/share.
  • Shares are being sold by a General Atlantic–affiliated entity (the selling shareholder).
  • DLocal will not receive any proceeds, as it is not selling shares in this offering.
  • The offering is expected to close on September 5, 2025.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to 2.25M additional shares from the selling shareholder.
  • DLO shares fall 5.5% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise declares $0.13 dividend
data.description:
• Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) declares $0.13/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.28%
  • Payable Oct. 17; for shareholders of record Sept. 18; ex-div Sept. 18.
  • See HPE Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Great Elm Capital launches public offering of unsecured notes due 2030
data.description:
• Great Elm Capital (NASDAQ:GECC) has begun an underwritten public offering of unsecured notes due 2030.
  • The notes are expected to list on Nasdaq under the symbol “GECCG” within 30 days of issuance.
  • Interest rate and terms will be finalized through negotiations with underwriters.
  • Proceeds will primarily be used to redeem 8.75% notes due 2028.
  • Remaining proceeds may be used to repurchase or redeem other outstanding notes, repay credit facility borrowings, or for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: FEMSA to control 100% of OXXO Brazil
data.description:
• Fomento Económico Mexicano (NYSE:FMX) said on Thursday that it has entered into definitive agreements with Raízen to amicably terminate their joint venture in Brazil known as “Grupo Nós” that includes OXXO proximity stores and Shell Select convenience stores. 
  • Under the terms of the agreement, FEMSA will retain all the OXXO stores in Brazil, as well as the distribution center located in Cajamar, São Paulo, while Raízen will retain all the Shell Select convenience stores. 
  • All other assets and liabilities will be allocated between Raízen and FEMSA as agreed in the definitive agreements. 
  • The transaction will be cash-neutral for both parties, with FEMSA assuming the existing and outstanding debt of Grupo Nós as of closing.
  • Completion of the separation of the OXXO stores and the Shell Select convenience stores is subject to regulatory approvals and other customary conditions and is expected to close in the coming months.

data.title: W.P. Carey YTD investment volume totals $1.3B, nearing full-year guidance range
data.description:
W.P. Carey (NYSE:WPC) completed additional investments of $250.8M after Q2, bringing its year-to-date investment volume to ~$1.3B and on the verge of attaining its full-year guidance of $1.4B-$1.8B, the company said on Thursday.

Dispositions in Q3-to-date generated gross proceeds of $310.0M, bringing year-to-date disposition volume to $875.0M. That compares with its full-year disposition volume guidance of $900M-$1.3B.

The acquisitions and dispositions illustrate the net-lease REIT's portfolio repositioning. The acquisitions included mostly single-tenant properties in North America and Europe, while its sales were primarily self-storage operating properties.

Gross proceeds from the sale of self-storage operating properties now total $460.8M for the year, representing half of the company's self-storage operating portfolio net operating income at the start of 2025.

On the strength of its YTD activity and its pipeline, the company sees a path towards the high end of its guidance range if it continues to execute at similar levels. W.P. Carey (NYSE:WPC) will review its guidance assumptions when it reports Q3 earnings, its CEO said.

"We continue to demonstrate our ability to source and close deals in the current environment, at cap rates, lease terms, and rent escalations in-line with where we've been investing throughout this year," said CEO and President Jason Fox. "Given our current strategy of funding investments primarily through accretive sales of non-core assets, this has generated approximately 150 basis points of spread between the average cap rates on our dispositions and new investments."

data.title: 1-800 FLOWERS.COM slips deeper into the red as sales continue to slip
data.description:
1-800-FLOWERS.COM (NASDAQ:FLWS) slipped further into the red as the company continued to struggle with shifting consumer expectations, evolving competition, and “fast-moving” technology, resulting in a wider-than-expected loss in the fiscal fourth quarter.

“We haven’t fully lived up to our potential in recent years,” CEO Adolfo Villagomez said.

“We’re making the company leaner and more agile, putting the customer at the center of everything we do, and using data to make smarter decisions…at the same time driving operational discipline, efficiency, and accountability to get back to growth, deliver a better experience, and create long-term value for shareholders,” he added.

Following disappointing results for the prior quarter which included a 12.6% drop in revenue year-over-year and compressed margins, the company experienced another 6.7% drop in revenue to $336.6M, which beat Wall Street’s conservative expectations of $330M.

Profits, however, continued to erode with the company reporting an adjusted loss of $0.69 per share from a loss of $0.34 per share last year and $0.18 worse than anticipated. Adjusted EBITDA loss for the quarter was $24.2M versus a loss of $8.8M in the same quarter last year. Gross margin decreased 290 basis points to 35.5%, largely due to a highly promotional sales environment and deleveraging on the sales decline.

On the balance sheet, the company had negative free cash flow of $67.8M versus positive FCF of $56.4M at the end of June 2024 as net cash provided by operating activities deteriorated to $(26.4M) from $95M a year ago.

The company did not provide FQ1 or FY26 guidance.

Shares of 1-800-FLOWERS.COM (NASDAQ:FLWS) were trading more than 12% lower in premarket action.

data.title: Marriott Vacations announces offering of senior notes
data.description:
• Marriott Vacations (NYSE:VAC) said on Wednesday that its wholly owned subsidiary, Marriott Ownership Resorts, intends to offer $575M aggregate principal amount of senior notes due 2033. 
  • The issuer intends to use the net proceeds from the notes (i) for the payment of $575M aggregate principal amount of the 2026 Convertible Notes due at or prior to maturity on January 15, 2026 and (ii) to pay transaction expenses and fees in connection with the foregoing.
  • Marriott Vacations (NYSE:VAC) shares were down 0.77% in premarket trading.

data.title: Sekisui House reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Sekisui House, Ltd. press release (OTCPK:SKHSY): Q2 Operating profit of ¥155.4B
  • Revenue of ¥2.02T (+8.6% Y/Y).
